marks.label: Keel of spiny scutes on belly
marks.description: A row of small, sharp scutes runs along the belly keel. Run a finger along it and you feel them. The most reliable sprat tell.

marks.label: No dark spot behind gill
marks.description: Unlike the herring, the sprat has no dark spot at the shoulder. A small (to ~15 cm), slim, bright-silver baitfish.

marks.label: Single small dorsal fin
marks.description: One short dorsal fin over the middle of the back, not forward of the pelvic fins as in the similar herring.

marks.label: Bright orange spots
marks.description: Scattered bright orange-red spots over a smooth brown upper side, the plaice's signature, separating it from dab and flounder.

marks.label: Both eyes on one side
marks.description: A flatfish: both eyes sit on the right (upper) side. The underside it lies on is plain white.

marks.label: Bony ridge behind eyes
marks.description: A short row of 4-7 bony knobs runs from behind the eyes; smooth skin otherwise.

marks.label: Bony tubercles along the fin bases
marks.description: A rough run of small bony knobs along the bases of the dorsal and anal fins and the lateral line, rough to the touch unlike the smooth plaice.

marks.label: Pale, scattered spots
marks.description: Any orange spots are pale and scattered, never the vivid, well-defined orange of a plaice.

marks.label: Dull brown estuary flatfish
marks.description: A dull greenish-brown right-eyed flatfish; the one UK flatfish that runs up into brackish estuaries and tidal rivers.

marks.label: Long cheek spine
marks.description: A long spine on the cheek (preopercular) reaches back past the gill opening, the tell vs the short-spined sea scorpion.

marks.label: Big spiny head, fan pectorals
marks.description: A broad, spiny, knobbly head and large fan-like pectoral fins; a stout, tapering body.

marks.label: Mottled camouflage
marks.description: Highly variable blotchy camouflage (green, brown, reddish), sitting motionless among weed and rock.

marks.label: Bright silver lateral stripe
marks.description: A very bright, solid silver stripe runs the whole length of the flank, the sand smelt's most conspicuous feature.

marks.label: Translucent greenish body
marks.description: A slender, semi-transparent body with a greenish tinge above the silver stripe; a small (to ~15 cm), near-surface schooler.

marks.label: Two small dorsal fins
marks.description: Two small, separate dorsal fins; the first is tiny and spiny; both are set well back on the body.

marks.label: Flat-headed, sits on sand
marks.description: A small flat-headed dragonet that lies pressed to sandy-muddy bottoms with eyes set high on top of the head. Smaller than the common dragonet.

marks.label: Cream-and-brown spotty camouflage
marks.description: Finely spotted and reticulated (net-patterned) in cream and brown for camouflage on sand, hence the spotted dragonet.

marks.label: Modest first dorsal (male)
marks.description: The male raises a first dorsal fin in display, but it is lower and less filamentous than the tall sail of the common dragonet.

marks.label: Long chin barbel
marks.description: A long single whisker under the chin, distinctly longer than bib's; the most reliable cod feature.

marks.label: Pale lateral line
marks.description: A distinct off-white stripe curves along the flank, easiest to see against the mottled back. No sharp kink.

marks.label: Mottled olive-brown back
marks.description: Peppered, mottled camouflage on a green-brown base; cleaner-coloured pollack and saithe lack this speckling.

marks.label: Thick fleshy lips
marks.description: Big, rubbery lips for picking shellfish off the rock, a wrasse hallmark, strongest in the chunky ballan.

marks.label: Single long dorsal fin
marks.description: One continuous dorsal fin runs almost the whole back (spiny at the front, soft behind), not split like a bass or gadoid.

marks.label: Stout mottled body
marks.description: A deep, robust green-to-brown body, often pale-spotted and highly variable. Our largest wrasse.

marks.label: Dark spot behind pectoral fin
marks.description: The first of the two namesake dark spots sits just behind the pectoral fin.

marks.label: Dark spot at the tail base
marks.description: The second spot sits at the tail base. Two spots plus orange-brown with blue speckles equals two-spotted goby.

marks.label: Hovers in midwater shoals
marks.description: Unlike other gobies it does not perch on the bottom; it hovers in loose midwater groups over kelp and weed.

marks.label: Dark spot on upper tail base
marks.description: A bold black spot at the TOP of the tail base, the goldsinny's signature mark.

marks.label: Dark spot at front of dorsal
marks.description: A second dark blotch at the very front of the dorsal fin. The two black spots together clinch it.

marks.label: Small reddish-brown body
marks.description: Our smallest common wrasse (to ~15 cm), slender and reddish-brown, hugging rocky crevices.

marks.label: Wavy green-blue bars
marks.description: Bold, wavy, tiger-stripe bars across a metallic blue-green back, the most eye-catching fish in UK waters.

marks.label: Streamlined forked tail
marks.description: A deeply forked tail and slim, torpedo-shaped body built for sustained fast swimming.

marks.label: Silver-white belly
marks.description: A plain silver-white belly with no bars (the bars only run down the back), sharp contrast with the coloured back.

marks.label: Curved lateral line over pectoral
marks.description: A distinct concave arch in the lateral line just above the pectoral fin, the dab's single most reliable tell against plaice and flounder.

marks.label: Rough sandpaper skin
marks.description: The scales feel rough and sandpapery to the touch; the only common UK flatfish with this texture.

marks.label: Plain sandy-brown, right-eyed
marks.description: Both eyes on the right (upper) side; a plain sandy-brown back with no bright orange spots, plainer than plaice.

marks.label: Blue-green lines on the face
marks.description: A maze of blue-green wavy lines over the cheek and gill cover, vivid on breeding males.

marks.label: Dark spot on the tail base
marks.description: A small dark spot in the centre of the tail base, present in both sexes; the most reliable corkwing tell.

marks.label: Comma behind the eye
marks.description: A short dark comma-shaped mark just behind the eye (clearest on males).

marks.label: Two long chin barbels
marks.description: A pair of long sensory barbels under the chin, used to probe the sand for food, the red mullet's standout feature.

marks.label: Reddish, with stripes/blotches
marks.description: Pink-red flanks marked with reddish-brown stripes and blotches; can flush brighter red. A steeply-sloped forehead.

marks.label: Two separate dorsal fins
marks.description: Two well-separated dorsal fins on a deep-bellied body that grubs along sandy and gravelly bottoms.

marks.label: Straight lateral line
marks.description: The pale line down the flank is nearly straight, with no sharp upward kink over the pectoral fin (that kink is pollack's tell).

marks.label: Level jaw
marks.description: Upper and lower jaws are about equal (pollack's lower jaw juts out). Barbel tiny or absent.

marks.label: Dark greenish back
marks.description: A dark green-brown back over silvery sides, schooling. Also called coalfish or coley.
